title: Fedora gating test failure: 312 sdnotify : play kube - with policies
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
```
not ok 312 sdnotify : play kube - with policies
# (from function `run_podman' in file ./helpers.bash, line 207,
#  in test file ./260-sdnotify.bats, line 341)
#   `run_podman container wait $container_a' failed
# # /usr/bin/podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# # /usr/bin/podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
# # /usr/bin/podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
# quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 f26aa69bb3f3

# Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/fedora:31...

# Storing signatures
# a7a37f74ff864eec55891b64ad360d07020827486e30a92ea81d16459645b26a

# Error: no container with name or ID "test_pod-a" found: no such container

# CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED                 STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
# 0de3cb821f7e  localhost/podman-pause:4.3.0-1666271887              Less than a second ago  Created                 9cffc8377ee1-service

# 68831fd785b8  quay.io/libpod/fedora:31                             Less than a second ago  Created                 test_pod-a

# 139810

# *** TIMED OUT ***

# a43fed04129ef695d2e4fb2db4ed61e529e47c47585b8c3a83fce65b65f424c8
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Fedora gating tests


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
4.3.0-2
```

comments.body: This is #16076, which ~~was~~ should've been fixed a week ago in #16118. @vrothberg PTAL. @lsm5 please confirm that this is 4.3, not something old.

> This is #16076, which ~was~ should've been fixed a week ago in #16118. @vrothberg PTAL. @lsm5 please confirm that this is 4.3, not something old.

It's the latest v4.3.0 on rawhide https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2022-48156b03b5

> This is #16076, which ~was~ should've been fixed a week ago in #16118. @vrothberg PTAL. @lsm5 please confirm that this is 4.3, not something old.

The "fix" is part of v4.3.0 but apparently not sufficient.  Any chance we can restart gating tests?

Did we see the test flake in rawhide before?

> The "fix" is part of v4.3.0 but apparently not sufficient. Any chance we can restart gating tests?
> 
> Did we see the test flake in rawhide before?

`before` meaning before v4.3.0 or on a prior test run with v4.3.0 ? Maybe @edsantiago remembers better.

> before meaning before v4.3.0 or on a prior test run with v4.3.0 ? Maybe @edsantiago remembers better.

In general.  Any previous of version of Podman but this test.

> In general. Any previous of version of Podman but this test.

ack, no recollection. I'll leave it to Ed.

There is no mechanism (push nor pull) for me to know of Fedora gating-test results, so I can neither monitor them nor save logs. I'm sorry, I have no idea what the history of this flake is. Since @vrothberg _just_ fixed it a week ago in `main`, I think the important consideration is that the fix in #16118 may not be sufficient.
